title: rubus ursinus subsp. macropetalus
intro: Rubus ursinus subsp. macropetalus, also known as the Pacific salmonberry, is a plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a subspecies of the Black Raspberry (Rubus ursinus). The medicinal properties of Rubus ursinus subsp. macropetalus include its high antioxidant content and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. This plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and wounds, with its health benefits including reducing inflammation, fighting cancer cells, and supporting cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of Rubus ursinus subsp. macropetalus include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
